fields.description:
## Context
The first **asymmetric** scheme for the attest substrate (epic brilliant-curly-songbird): sign/verify over attestation bytes via OpenSSH `ssh-keygen -Y` (stdlib `subprocess`, **no new core dependency**), verified offline against an `allowed_signers`-format trust input, reusing developers existing SSH keys. Plugs into the registry `Scheme` protocol (task 518b). The `src/rebar/attest/` package already exists (task 9fd5).

## Mechanism (empirically confirmed in this env, OpenSSH 10.2)
- **Sign:** `ssh-keygen -Y sign -f <privkey> -n <namespace>` reads the payload on **stdin** and writes the **armored SSHSIG to stdout** (argv array, no shell). `sign(pae_bytes, key_path, namespace)` returns those stdout bytes.
- **Verify:** `ssh-keygen -Y verify -f <allowed_signers_file> -I <principal> -n <namespace> -s <sig_file>` reads the payload on **stdin**; the signature must be a **file on disk** (`-s`). Both the allowed_signers content and the signature are written to temp files; the process branches **only on exit code** (0 = valid). Argv array, never a shell string.
- **Validity windows:** enforced via `valid-before=`/`valid-after=` options on the `allowed_signers` line; `ssh-keygen -Y verify` honors them against the current time (or `-Overify-time`). This support requires **OpenSSH >= 8.9** â€” that is the reason for the version floor (the `-Y sign/verify` subcommands themselves exist since 8.0, but allowed_signers validity intervals landed in 8.9).
- **Never** use `-Y check-novalidate` (structure-only) for authorization.

## Files
- `src/rebar/attest/sshsig.py` â€” `ssh_keygen_version()`, `ensure_available()`, `sign()`, `SshsigScheme` (implements the registry `Scheme` protocol; `trust_root` = allowed_signers content, `signatures[0].keyid` = principal, `signatures[0].sig` = armored SSHSIG bytes), `register_sshsig_scheme()`.
- `src/rebar/attest/__init__.py` â€” call `register_sshsig_scheme()` at import.
- `tests/unit/test_attest_sshsig.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SshsigScheme.verify(pae_bytes, signatures, namespace, trust_root)` invokes `ssh-keygen -Y verify` via an **argv array** (no shell), feeds `pae_bytes` on stdin, writes `trust_root` (allowed_signers) and `signatures[0].sig` to temp files, allowlist-validates the principal + namespace (non-empty, no control chars), and returns a `registry.Verdict` branching **only** on the process exit code (0 -> `certified`, non-zero -> `mismatch`).
- [ ] Fail-closed test matrix (real `ssh-keygen`): `verified is False` on **tampered bytes**, **wrong namespace**, **unknown principal**, **substituted key** (allowed_signers lists a different key for the principal), and **expired validity window** (`valid-before` in the past). Plus a happy round-trip: `sign()` then `verify()` -> `certified`.
- [ ] `ssh_keygen_version()` detects the OpenSSH `(major, minor)`; `SshsigScheme.verify` **fails closed** (a non-`certified` `Verdict`, never a silent pass) when ssh-keygen is absent or `< 8.9` â€” test monkeypatches version detection to `None` and asserts `verified is False`. `ensure_available()` raises `SshKeygenUnavailable` with a clear message on absence/too-old.
- [ ] `register_sshsig_scheme()` registers `SshsigScheme` into the registry (discoverable via `registry.get_scheme("sshsig")`); no POLICY kinds are pinned here (authorship kinds are the identity epic).
